description: Got a data pipeline at /home/user/reports — bunch of python scripts that clean CSVs then aggregate them. Running them manually every time is getting old. Need a Makefile so I can just type `make` and it runs clean.py on each csv in raw/, then runs aggregate.py on all the cleaned outputs. clean.py takes input and output paths as args, aggregate.py just reads everything in cleaned/ and writes to final/summary.csv.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/reports/ exists and is writable
- /home/user/reports/raw/ contains: sales_q1.csv, sales_q2.csv, inventory.csv (three CSV files with dummy data)
- /home/user/reports/cleaned/ exists and is empty
- /home/user/reports/final/ exists and is empty
- /home/user/reports/clean.py exists and works: `python clean.py <input> <output>` reads a CSV, strips whitespace from headers, and writes to output path
- /home/user/reports/aggregate.py exists and works: `python aggregate.py` reads all CSVs in cleaned/, concatenates them, writes to final/summary.csv
- Python 3.x and make are installed
- No Makefile exists yet

Expected final state:
- /home/user/reports/Makefile exists
- Running `cd /home/user/reports && make` exits 0
- After make runs: /home/user/reports/cleaned/sales_q1.csv, cleaned/sales_q2.csv, and cleaned/inventory.csv all exist
- After make runs: /home/user/reports/final/summary.csv exists and contains data from all three cleaned files
- Running `make` a second time with no file changes should do nothing (proper dependency tracking)

Invariants:
- clean.py and aggregate.py unchanged
- raw/*.csv files unchanged
- Directory structure preserved

Anti-shortcut guards:
- Makefile must use pattern rules or explicit rules that invoke clean.py and aggregate.py (not a shell script that ignores make's dependency system)
- `grep -E 'clean\.py|aggregate\.py' /home/user/reports/Makefile` must match (the Makefile must actually call these scripts)
